name: issue-queue-state-machine
prompt:
Implement the Issue Queue state machine.

In agentrail/afk/queue_state.py expose:
- QueueEntry — a dataclass with fields number, tier, remaining_budget, state, blocked_by; state defaults to QueueState.QUEUED and blocked_by defaults to empty. Its single state field holds a QueueState while the entry is live and a Terminal once it terminates, so dataclasses.replace and isinstance(entry.state, Terminal) both work.
- QueueState enum: QUEUED, RUNNING, PARKED
- Terminal enum: GREEN, ESCALATED_TO_HUMAN, BLOCKED
- Tier enum: CHEAP, STRONG
- Event enum with exactly these members: START, GATE_GREEN, GATE_RED, SECURITY_BLOCK (the input alphabet that drives transitions; the enum must be iterable over these members)
- admit(entry, open_blockers) -> QueueEntry
- transition(entry, event) -> QueueEntry

Intent: admit decides whether a newly-seen issue is parked or queued, and transition drives a running issue through gate outcomes. remaining_budget is an integer retry counter, and a red gate spends exactly one unit of it (decrement by 1). start is valid only on a QUEUED entry; applied to any other state it raises ValueError. A green gate on a running entry terminates GREEN. A security block on a running entry escalates to a human regardless of remaining budget or tier. A red gate on a running entry escalates it to a stronger tier and decrements the budget by one, leaving it QUEUED to retry — but a red gate at the strongest tier, or once the budget is exhausted, escalates to a human instead. admit parks an issue when its blocked_by set intersects the open_blockers it is admitted against and otherwise queues it (an issue with no blockers queues even when unrelated blockers are open; re-admitting a parked issue whose blockers have cleared queues it). BLOCKED is a terminal reached only through the admission path, never produced by transition. A terminal entry is frozen: transition on a terminal entry returns it unchanged (an idempotent no-op, never raising). Every issue must reach a terminal state.
